pattern: Filter by the three constraints then sort by (rating descending, id descending) — the problem specifies a deterministic total order so the result is unique.
hints: Apply the three filters independently: if veganFriendly is 1 skip any restaurant with veganFriendlyi == 0; always skip any with price > maxPrice or distance > maxDistance.
After filtering, sort the survivors by rating descending; break ties by id descending.
Extract only the id field from each surviving entry to build the output list.

edge_inputs.description: vegan filter removes all but one
no vegan filter, all pass price and distance
tight price and distance filter leaves only two
all restaurants filtered out — empty result
tie in rating resolved by higher id first

stress.description: large random restaurant list with random filter values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    ids = rng.sample(range(1, 100001), min(n, 100000))
    restaurants = [[ids[i], rng.randint(1, 100000), rng.randint(0, 1), rng.randint(1, 100000), rng.randint(1, 100000)] for i in range(len(ids))]
    vegan = rng.randint(0, 1)
    maxPrice = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    maxDist = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    return [restaurants, vegan, maxPrice, maxDist]
